functional_purpose: Simulation-only BFM wrapper for master verification

reset.name: rst_n
reset.deassertion: synchronized independently into clk and sclk domains
reset_rules: Any sclk or cs_n activity during reset shall be ignored
No completion or rx_valid pulse may be generated from partial pre-reset state
Both domains resume from idle after synchronized release

parameters.name: DEFAULT_TX_BYTE
parameters.description: Byte shifted when no valid TX preload is available

frame_start.description: Active frame begins only on first counted rising sclk edge after cs_n was previously high and legal-start conditions are met
frame_start.pending_start_interval: Between cs_n falling and first counted rising edge

frame_complete.description: Frame completes on the 8th qualified rising edge

abort.description: If cs_n rises before 8th qualified rising edge, frame is aborted and completion is suppressed

frame_rearm.description: A new frame cannot begin until cs_n returns high and then low again

first_bit_rule.description: At legal frame start, selected transmit bit[7] shall already be present on miso_o and miso_oe asserted before the first counted rising edge

post_complete_rule.description: Additional sclk activity while cs_n remains low after normal completion shall be ignored

external_assumptions: cs_n low and stable before first counted rising edge for legal start
cs_n remains low through that first counted rising edge
MOSI satisfies setup/hold to qualified rising edges at package boundary
T_CSS_MIN is an external requirement, not internally measured by DUT

miso_oe.inactive_behavior: When 0, MISO has no electrical effect; miso_o may retain prior value

tx_path.description: sclk-domain frame-start logic must use coherent shadow copy of tx preload; direct sampling of clk-domain tx_data/tx_valid is forbidden

rx_path.description: reported clk-domain completion must correspond to one coherent received byte; if events collapse before observation, only latest completed byte must be preserved

constraints_affecting_tb_structure: Testbench must model clk and sclk as independent domains
Testbench must distinguish legal timing cases from unspecified setup/hold violations
Core bring-up should precede BFM-only feature verification
Early checking should prefer frame-level directed reference behavior over a complex scoreboard

items.ambiguous_requirement: Which module should be primary target for first TB bring-up
items.why_it_matters: Architecture and checker scope differ between spi_slave_core and spi_slave_bfm
items.planning_assumption: Bring-up starts with spi_slave_core; spi_slave_bfm is a later extension

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact clk and sclk frequency relationship is not specified
items.why_it_matters: CDC-sensitive checks and rx_valid timing observation depend on relative rates
items.planning_assumption: Start with stable independent clocks and clk comfortably faster than sclk, then vary later

items.ambiguous_requirement: T_CSS_MIN and cs-high re-arm timing are externally required but not numerically defined here
items.why_it_matters: Legal vs illegal start classification depends on them
items.planning_assumption: Legal tests use clearly separated timing; illegal-start tests use only digitally obvious violations away from exclusion windows

items.ambiguous_requirement: Internal state visibility for frame_active/frame_complete/bit_count is unspecified
items.why_it_matters: Strong internal state assertions may not be portable across implementations
items.planning_assumption: External black-box checking is primary; internal visibility is optional debug aid only

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact rx_valid timing wording may be interpreted differently at the clk-domain observation boundary
items.why_it_matters: Off-by-one-cycle failures are likely if checker meaning is wrong
items.planning_assumption: Treat rx_valid as a deterministic one-cycle clk pulse with strict timing per contract, but keep checker modular

items.ambiguous_requirement: Whether collapsed multiple completions must be tested early
items.why_it_matters: This is a complex lossy CDC corner that can obscure simpler bugs
items.planning_assumption: Defer collapse-stress behavior to a late stage

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact value significance of miso_o when miso_oe is 0
items.why_it_matters: Inactive data should not cause false failures
items.planning_assumption: Strongly check miso_oe inactivity; strongly check miso_o only when enabled or explicitly reset-defined

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact immediacy of miso_oe deassertion when cs_n rises
items.why_it_matters: Overly cycle-exact assertions may be brittle
items.planning_assumption: Enforce miso_oe inactive when cs_n is high, but do not overconstrain sub-cycle timing in early TB

items.why_it_matters: Negative tests need a reproducible condition outside unspecified timing windows
items.planning_assumption: Use clean digital violations where cs_n is not stably low across the first attempted counted rising edge

items.ambiguous_requirement: Whether next-frame response is intrinsic core behavior or environment-driven use case
items.why_it_matters: Could incorrectly imply autonomous response generation inside core
items.planning_assumption: For spi_slave_core, verify only that later preload can affect a later frame, not that RX automatically feeds TX

items.ambiguous_requirement: Required post-reset settling interval before legal traffic
items.why_it_matters: Immediately driving traffic after reset release may create ambiguous results with synchronized deassertion
items.planning_assumption: Wait several clk and sclk cycles after reset release before nominal traffic

items.ambiguous_requirement: BFM anomaly injection behavior may be only partially specified
items.why_it_matters: Late-stage checks may need to be weaker and more observational
items.planning_assumption: BFM anomaly tests verify compatibility with nominal semantics and presence of expected hooks/logging, not deep micro-behavior

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact post-completion held value of miso_o after extra clocks
items.why_it_matters: Spec strongly prohibits further shifting but does not force a new data value
items.planning_assumption: Check that no further MISO advancement occurs, not a particular held bit value

items.ambiguous_requirement: Whether CDC mechanism must literally be toggle-based
items.why_it_matters: Checker should verify contract, not a single implementation style
items.planning_assumption: Verify observable coherent-selection and coherent-reporting behavior only

items.ambiguous_requirement: Exact semantic contract of BFM queue consumption and expectation-hook failure reporting is only partly implied by the spec text
items.why_it_matters: A weak BFM test could prove hook wiring without proving that the helper APIs preserve nominal semantics or report mismatches correctly
items.planning_assumption: Verify observable per-frame queue consumption order, nominal semantic equivalence against direct pin driving, and both pass/fail expectation-hook paths without constraining internal logging implementation

philosophy: Verify reset and idle behavior before transactions
Verify one legal minimal frame before multiple frames
Verify nominal completion before abort and invalid cases
Verify simple deterministic data patterns before stress and corner cases
Introduce only one major new complexity axis per stage where possible
Promote exact clk-domain reporting checks out of optional stress and into the main correctness path

stages.name: testbench_infrastructure_sanity
stages.purpose: Prove clocks, reset, connectivity, and simulation observations work before protocol debugging

stages.name: reset_and_idle_behavior
stages.purpose: Prove deterministic reset values and idle behavior with cs_n high

stages.name: minimum_legal_transaction_frame
stages.purpose: Verify one legal 8-bit frame with preloaded TX and one receive completion

stages.name: clk_domain_report_contract
stages.purpose: Verify exact rx_valid latency, pulse width, and rx_data stability obligations in the clk domain

stages.name: basic_functional_progression
stages.purpose: Add default-TX fallback and frame-start TX immutability

stages.name: representative_data_path_checks
stages.purpose: Add data-pattern confidence for MSB-first mapping and edge alignment

stages.name: boundary_and_corner_cases
stages.purpose: Add 8th-edge completion boundary and post-completion extra-clock ignore behavior

stages.name: abort_reset_invalid_sequence_handling
stages.purpose: Add abort, reset-during-frame, and illegal-start behavior

stages.name: back_to_back_and_repeated_operation
stages.purpose: Add repeated legal frame operation, next-frame effects, and frame-to-frame separation checks

stages.name: stress_and_bfm_semantics
stages.purpose: Add clock-ratio variation, collapse behavior, queue semantics, and BFM-only extension checks

stages.why_now: Without a trusted harness, later failures are hard to localize
expected_observables: clk toggles
sclk can be controlled independently
rst_n assertion and release are observable
outputs settle to known values after reset

planned_tests.objective: Prove the simulation harness can start, drive both clocks, and perform reset cleanly
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run clk and sclk, assert reset, release reset, keep SPI otherwise idle
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Outputs match reset-defined values during reset and remain deterministic after release
key_pass_fail_criteria: no fatal or persistent unknowns
miso_oe == 0 during reset
miso_o == 0 during reset
rx_valid == 0 during reset
rx_data == 8'h00 during reset

planned_tests.objective: Verify DUT outputs are visible and legal in idle state
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Hold cs_n high, cycle clocks, release reset, observe outputs
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Idle outputs remain deterministic and legal
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_valid remains 0
miso_oe remains 0 when cs_n is high
no X or Z on synthesizable outputs after reset release

stages.why_now: Reset and idle bugs contaminate all later transaction tests
expected_observables: exact reset values
no protocol effect from SPI activity during reset
no frame effect while cs_n is high
miso_oe inactive when unselected

planned_tests.objective: Verify visible reset state and clean post-reset idle state
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Assert reset, release reset, keep cs_n high, no transaction
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Exact reset values during reset; idle behavior after release
key_pass_fail_criteria: outputs match spec during reset
rx_valid stays 0 after release while idle
miso_oe stays 0 after release while cs_n is high

planned_tests.objective: Ensure sclk/cs_n/mosi activity during reset does not create latent completion
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Toggle SPI pins while rst_n is asserted, then release reset
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No completion event or residual protocol effect from reset-period traffic
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_valid never pulses due to reset-period activity
post-release DUT starts cleanly from idle

planned_tests.objective: Verify no transaction progresses while chip select is inactive
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Hold cs_n high and toggle sclk with varying mosi
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No receive completion and no active MISO drive
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_valid remains 0
miso_oe remains 0

stages.why_now: Smallest legal end-to-end protocol behavior with strongest debug value
expected_observables: miso_oe asserted for selected frame
miso_o presents selected tx bit7 before first counted rising edge
mosi assembles into one rx byte
exactly one rx_valid pulse occurs

planned_tests.objective: Verify one complete legal frame using known TX preload and known MOSI byte
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Publish valid tx preload well before frame start and drive one clean 8-bit legal frame
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Preloaded byte shifts out MSB-first, MOSI byte is captured, one rx_valid pulse occurs
key_pass_fail_criteria: correct MISO bit sequence
correct rx_data value
exactly one rx_valid pulse
no extra completion

planned_tests.objective: Isolate and verify the first-bit launch requirement at legal frame start
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Preload a byte with distinct bit7, assert cs_n legally, observe miso_o and miso_oe before first rising edge
planned_tests.expected_behavior: miso_o already equals selected tx bit7 and miso_oe is already asserted before first counted rising edge
key_pass_fail_criteria: first-bit value correct before first rising edge
miso_oe active before first rising edge

stages.why_now: The spec makes clk-domain reporting behavior normative; catching off-by-one CDC/reporting bugs early prevents false confidence from later byte-level tests.
expected_observables: rx_valid asserts for exactly one clk cycle
rx_valid occurs exactly one clk edge after the completed frame is reported into the clk-domain interface contract
rx_data remains stable for the full clk cycle in which rx_valid is 1

planned_tests.objective: Verify one completed legal frame produces exactly one rx_valid pulse with exact clk-domain latency per contract.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run one legal frame with clocks chosen so the sclk-domain completion and clk edges are easy to observe and correlate.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: One and only one rx_valid pulse occurs, and it occurs on the required clk edge relative to reported completion.
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_valid pulse width is exactly one clk cycle
no early rx_valid assertion
no late rx_valid assertion
no second rx_valid pulse for the same frame

planned_tests.objective: Verify rx_data is coherent and stable for the entire clk cycle in which rx_valid is asserted.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Complete one legal frame with an asymmetric byte and sample rx_data throughout the rx_valid-high cycle.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: rx_data matches the completed byte and remains unchanged while rx_valid is 1.
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_data matches the expected received byte
rx_data is stable for the full clk cycle where rx_valid == 1

planned_tests.objective: Verify one completed frame cannot create more than one clk-domain pulse even when observation windows are stretched.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Complete one legal frame, continue clk running, and monitor long enough to detect any duplicate reporting.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Exactly one rx_valid pulse is observed for the frame.
key_pass_fail_criteria: one frame completion maps to at most one rx_valid pulse
rx_data remains the completed byte until any later independent completion

stages.why_now: Adds alternate TX source handling only after nominal frame behavior and clk-domain reporting are already trusted.
expected_observables: DEFAULT_TX_BYTE used when tx_valid is 0
same-frame TX source does not change after frame start

planned_tests.objective: Verify transmit fallback when no valid preload exists
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Keep tx_valid low and drive one legal frame
planned_tests.expected_behavior: MISO shifts DEFAULT_TX_BYTE while RX still captures MOSI normally
key_pass_fail_criteria: observed MISO bits equal DEFAULT_TX_BYTE
rx_data matches MOSI byte
one rx_valid pulse occurs

planned_tests.objective: Verify current frame is not affected by tx_data or tx_valid changes after frame-start selection
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Start frame with preload A, then change tx_data and tx_valid mid-frame
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Current frame continues shifting originally selected byte only
key_pass_fail_criteria: no mid-frame MISO corruption due to later tx changes

stages.why_now: After core mechanics and reporting are trusted, pattern variety can isolate bit-order and edge mistakes.
expected_observables: correct bit ordering on RX and TX
no bit reversal or phase slip

planned_tests.objective: Confirm all-zero pattern handling
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: MOSI 8'h00 and preload TX 8'h00
planned_tests.expected_behavior: RX and TX both reflect all-zero pattern correctly
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_data == 8'h00
MISO sequence is all zero while enabled

planned_tests.objective: Confirm all-one pattern handling
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: MOSI 8'hFF and preload TX 8'hFF
planned_tests.expected_behavior: RX and TX both reflect all-one pattern correctly
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_data == 8'hFF
MISO sequence is all one while enabled

planned_tests.objective: Detect bit-position and shift-order mistakes
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use one-hot data patterns on RX and TX paths
planned_tests.expected_behavior: One-hot position is preserved exactly MSB-first
key_pass_fail_criteria: exact bit location is preserved

planned_tests.objective: Detect edge-alignment issues
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use alternating patterns such as 8'hAA and 8'h55
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Alternation is preserved exactly
key_pass_fail_criteria: no one-bit phase slip

planned_tests.objective: Specifically isolate first-bit and last-bit mapping correctness
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use asymmetric bytes such as 8'h81 or 8'h96
planned_tests.expected_behavior: First sampled bit maps to bit7 and eighth to bit0
key_pass_fail_criteria: exact mapping match

planned_tests.objective: Enforce the edge-accurate MISO transition restriction rather than inferring it only from byte sequences.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run a legal frame with a byte that causes multiple MISO transitions and observe miso_o across all relevant and irrelevant edges.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: miso_o changes on qualified falling edges only after legal frame start; it remains stable across qualified rising edges.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no MISO transition occurs on a qualified rising edge
all expected MISO transitions align with qualified falling edges only

stages.why_now: Adds completion-boundary behavior without mixing in abort or reset complexity.
expected_observables: completion occurs on 8th qualified rising edge
extra clocks do not create extra progress
frame re-arm requires cs_n high then low

planned_tests.objective: Confirm exact completion boundary on the 8th qualified rising edge
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Drive 8-bit legal frame with an identifiable final bit
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Final bit is captured and frame completes exactly at bit 8
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_data includes correct final bit0
one completion event only

planned_tests.objective: Verify extra clocks while cs_n remains low do not create a new frame or extra completion
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Complete one frame, hold cs_n low, continue toggling sclk
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No second completion and no further MISO advancement attributable to extra clocks
key_pass_fail_criteria: exactly one rx_valid pulse
no second captured byte
no additional MISO shift progression

planned_tests.objective: Verify a new frame cannot start without deselecting and reselecting cs_n
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Attempt more clocks after one completed frame before toggling cs_n high, then run a legal second frame after deselect/reselect
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No second frame before cs_n high; second frame accepted after cs_n high then low
key_pass_fail_criteria: second legal completion occurs only after high-low cs_n cycle

planned_tests.objective: Separate MISO post-completion hold behavior from rx_valid counting so a byte-sequence-only checker cannot miss extra shifting.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Complete a frame with a byte that would visibly change if additional shifts occurred, then continue extra clocks with cs_n low.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: miso_o exhibits no further shift progression attributable to the extra clocks once completion has occurred.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no post-completion MISO advancement is observed
no new frame start or receive completion occurs

stages.why_now: Nominal and boundary behavior are now stable enough that abnormal sequence failures can be localized cleanly.
expected_observables: aborted partial frame produces no rx_valid
reset kills in-flight frame with no latent completion
ignored illegal start does not activate frame behavior
legal recovery works after abort/reset

planned_tests.objective: Verify early cs_n deassertion aborts frame and suppresses completion
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Begin legal frame, shift fewer than 8 bits, then raise cs_n
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No rx_valid and no complete byte reported
key_pass_fail_criteria: rx_valid never pulses for the aborted frame

planned_tests.objective: Verify asynchronous reset dominates mid-frame and clears partial progress
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Begin legal frame, shift a few bits, assert rst_n low, then recover and run a clean legal frame
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Partial frame is discarded; post-reset behavior resumes from idle only
key_pass_fail_criteria: no completion from pre-reset partial frame
reset-defined outputs visible during reset
later legal frame passes

planned_tests.objective: Verify an attempted frame is ignored when cs_n is not low and stable soon enough before the first attempted counted rising edge.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Create a digitally obvious late-assertion violation away from package-boundary ambiguity.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No frame activation, no rx_valid, and miso_oe remains inactive until cs_n returns high and a later legal frame begins.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no rx_valid pulse for the illegal attempt
miso_oe remains 0 for the illegal attempt
later legal retry after cs_n high-low succeeds

planned_tests.objective: Verify an attempted frame is ignored when cs_n is not held low through the first attempted counted rising edge.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Deassert cs_n in a digitally obvious way before or through the first attempted rising edge, outside the exclusion window.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: The attempted start is ignored and the DUT remains idle until a later legal re-arm sequence.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no valid frame progression
bit counting does not manifest externally
later legal frame works only after cs_n returns high and falls low again

planned_tests.objective: Verify once a digitally illegal start is rejected, additional sclk activity while cs_n remains low is still ignored until explicit re-arm.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Cause an illegal start, continue toggling sclk with cs_n low, then re-arm with a clean high-low cycle and run a legal frame.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Ignored attempt remains ignored despite extra clocks; legal operation resumes only after re-arm.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no rx_valid during ignored interval
no active MISO drive during ignored interval
exactly one valid completion from the later legal frame only

planned_tests.objective: Prove an aborted frame does not poison a later legal transaction
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Abort one frame, deselect/reselect, then run a clean legal frame
planned_tests.expected_behavior: First frame suppressed, second frame completes normally
key_pass_fail_criteria: exactly one valid completion corresponding to the second frame only

stages.why_now: Repetition and next-frame behavior are stateful and are best checked after all single-frame outcomes are trusted.
expected_observables: one completion per legal frame
correct frame separation
preload changes affect later frame only

planned_tests.objective: Verify two complete frames separated by a clear cs_n high interval
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run frame A, deselect, optionally update preload, run frame B
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Two independent correct frame outcomes
key_pass_fail_criteria: exactly two correct completions

planned_tests.objective: Verify practical minimal re-arm behavior without assuming analog numbers not given in spec
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use a small but clean digital cs_n high gap between frames
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Next legal frame is accepted
key_pass_fail_criteria: both frames complete correctly

planned_tests.objective: Verify a new preload affects the next frame only
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use preload A for frame 1, publish preload B before frame 2, then run frame 2
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Frame 1 shifts A; frame 2 shifts B
key_pass_fail_criteria: exact per-frame MISO bytes match selected preloads

planned_tests.objective: Check stability across several directed legal frames
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run 4 to 8 legal frames with distinct TX/RX pairs
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Each frame remains independent and correct
key_pass_fail_criteria: all expected per-frame outcomes match
no missing or extra rx_valid pulses

planned_tests.objective: Verify a byte received in frame N can influence the transmit response of frame N+1 only, not the in-flight frame.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run frame N to receive a byte, update the next response through the intended environment/BFM path, then run frame N+1 and compare both frames.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Frame N uses its original selected transmit source; frame N+1 reflects the updated response source.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no retroactive effect on frame N transmit behavior
frame N+1 transmit behavior matches the intended next-frame response

stages.why_now: After core behavior and clk-domain contracts are trusted, stress and BFM semantics can be checked without obscuring first-failure localization.
expected_observables: nominal behavior remains correct across benign ratio changes
collapse tests honor latest-byte-only contract
BFM helper APIs preserve nominal protocol semantics
queue-driven and expectation-driven flows behave consistently with direct checkers
bug_localization_targets: mismatch between core semantics and BFM helper behavior
queue ordering or expectation-hook semantic bugs

planned_tests.objective: Verify nominal behavior is not tied to a single convenient clock ratio
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Re-run a subset of nominal tests across multiple clk:sclk ratios and phase offsets
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Same logical outcomes remain valid
key_pass_fail_criteria: selected nominal tests continue to pass across ratio sweep

planned_tests.objective: Verify the intentionally lossy non-backpressured RX contract under aggressive timing
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Drive multiple completed frames fast enough that clk-domain observation may collapse them
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Latest completed byte may be the only preserved one and at most one rx_valid pulse may occur for the collapsed set
key_pass_fail_criteria: checker accepts latest-only preservation
checker does not require one pulse per frame under collapse conditions

planned_tests.objective: Smoke-test BFM short-frame anomaly injection support
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use BFM API to inject a short frame anomaly
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Anomaly is injected and/or logged without redefining nominal protocol behavior
key_pass_fail_criteria: expected anomaly indication present
adjacent nominal tests remain valid

planned_tests.objective: Verify BFM extra-clock anomaly support aligns with core ignore-after-complete semantics
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Use BFM API to inject extra clocks after completion
planned_tests.expected_behavior: No second valid completion is created
key_pass_fail_criteria: anomaly path does not violate nominal ignore-after-complete rule

planned_tests.objective: Verify self-checking BFM expectation hook wiring after core behavior is trusted
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run one nominal frame through BFM expectation API
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Hook result agrees with direct external checkers
key_pass_fail_criteria: no mismatch between hook and direct observation

planned_tests.objective: Verify BFM task-driven nominal transactions preserve the same common protocol semantics as direct pin-level driving.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Execute equivalent nominal frames once through direct drivers and once through BFM helper APIs, then compare observations.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Observed frame boundaries, MISO behavior, RX results, and completion behavior are equivalent for nominal use.
key_pass_fail_criteria: no semantic mismatch between direct and BFM-driven nominal transfers

planned_tests.objective: Verify queued transmit bytes are consumed in frame order and do not corrupt nominal common semantics.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Queue multiple distinct TX bytes through the BFM API and run consecutive legal frames.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: Each frame consumes the expected queued byte in order; no frame consumes a later queued byte early.
key_pass_fail_criteria: per-frame MISO bytes match queue order exactly
queue consumption is one response per completed frame

planned_tests.objective: Verify the BFM expectation hook both passes correct traffic and detects intentional mismatches.
planned_tests.stimulus_concept: Run one matching expected receive case and one intentionally mismatched case through expect_rx_byte.
planned_tests.expected_behavior: The matching case passes cleanly and the mismatched case is reported by the expectation mechanism without redefining protocol semantics.
key_pass_fail_criteria: expected-pass case reports success
expected-fail case is detected and surfaced by the hook

generators.name: sclk_driver_or_generator
generators.role: transaction-controlled SPI clock with explicit edge counting; optional periodic mode for ratio sweep

generators.name: reset_driver
generators.role: reset-at-start and mid-frame reset support with controlled release waits

driver_helpers.name: apply_reset
driver_helpers.role: assert/deassert reset and wait for post-release settling

driver_helpers.name: publish_tx_preload
driver_helpers.role: drive tx_data and tx_valid under controlled stability assumptions

driver_helpers.name: drive_legal_spi_frame
driver_helpers.role: drive one legal 8-bit frame with configurable MOSI byte and legal cs lead time

driver_helpers.name: drive_illegal_start_attempt
driver_helpers.role: create digitally obvious illegal-start conditions away from exclusion windows

driver_helpers.name: drive_back_to_back_frames
driver_helpers.role: sequence multiple frames with controlled cs_n high spacing

driver_helpers.name: drive_bfm_nominal_frame
driver_helpers.role: exercise one nominal frame through the BFM task/API surface for semantic-equivalence checks

driver_helpers.name: queue_bfm_tx_bytes
driver_helpers.role: publish ordered BFM response bytes for queue-consumption tests

driver_helpers.name: expect_bfm_rx_byte
driver_helpers.role: invoke BFM expectation hooks for both positive and negative cases

monitors.name: spi_output_monitor
monitors.role: observe miso_o and miso_oe relative to sclk and cs_n to reconstruct transmitted sequence

monitors.name: rx_monitor
monitors.role: observe rx_valid pulse width, pulse timing, and sampled rx_data

monitors.name: protocol_event_monitor
monitors.role: infer frame boundaries, completion boundary, abort points, and extra-clock behavior from stimulus and outputs

monitors.name: clk_domain_report_monitor
monitors.role: correlate frame completion, rx_valid latency, and rx_data stability in the clk domain

rationale: enough to predict expected TX byte, RX byte, and whether completion should occur
avoids building a heavy scoreboard before repeated-frame stages
must also predict exact clk-domain reporting behavior for single-completion directed tests

useful_assertions: rx_valid pulse width is exactly one clk cycle
rx_valid never asserts for aborted frames
miso_oe is inactive when cs_n is high in core-focused tests
miso_o changes only on qualified falling edges during active frame driving
no second completion after extra clocks while cs_n remains low
first active MISO bit equals selected tx bit7 before first counted rising edge
no X or Z on synthesizable outputs after reset release
rx_valid asserts exactly one clk cycle after the completed frame is reported into the clk-domain interface contract
rx_data remains stable for the full clk cycle in which rx_valid is 1
miso_o does not change on qualified rising edges during active frame operation
after a digitally illegal start is ignored, additional sclk edges while cs_n remains low do not create frame progress until re-arm

strict_spec_driven: no X after reset release on synthesizable outputs
frame re-arm requires cs_n high then low
reset suppresses completion from partial pre-reset state
exact rx_valid latency for a single observed completion
rx_data stability throughout the valid cycle
MISO transition restriction to qualified falling edges
digitally illegal start remains ignored until cs_n re-arm
BFM queue order and expectation-hook pass/fail semantics at the observable API level

collapse behavior under aggressive CDC timing

strongly_verifiable_from_spec: RX bit mapping and MSB-first ordering

known_coverage_gaps: No strong functional coverage claim should be made for unspecified analog timing windows

BFM anomaly coverage should be separated from core functional coverage
A pure black-box TB cannot prove coherent shadow-copy implementation, only its observable contract
Structural requirements such as REQ-SPI-011 require explicit non-TB closure items and must not be marked closed by waveform checks alone

recommended_actions: Freeze the checker interpretation for exact rx_valid latency and rx_data stability before any code generation
Implement Stage 0 through Stage 3 first so clk-domain reporting bugs are caught before broader functional expansion
Treat REQ-SPI-011 as requiring both simulation and structural closure items

rationale: catches high-value off-by-one CDC/reporting bugs early
prevents TB-only closure claims for structural requirements
keeps BFM semantic debugging separate from core protocol debugging

major_strengths: Incremental stage ordering remains good for failure localization
